hawkish_phrases: did not expect that it would be appropriate to reduce the target range until they have gained greater confidence that inflation is moving sustainably toward 2 percent
Several participants observed that, were inflation to persist at an elevated level or to increase further, the target range for the federal funds rate might need to be raised
remained highly attentive to inflation risks
Risks to the inflation forecast were seen as tilted to the upside
dovish_phrases: there has been modest further progress toward the Committee's 2 percent inflation objective
the risks to achieving the Committee's employment and inflation goals have moved toward better balance
A number of participants remarked that monetary policy should stand ready to respond to unexpected economic weakness
lower- and moderate-income households were encountering increasing strains
some risk that further cooling in labor market conditions could be associated with an increased pace of layoffs
summary: The Committee unanimously held rates at 5.25-5.50 percent while acknowledging modest further progress on inflation but emphasizing they will not cut until gaining greater confidence inflation is moving sustainably toward 2 percent, with several participants noting rates might need to be raised if inflation persists.
